question: Binding-relevant [Ne]-core correlation differential between Na+H at infinity and NaH at R_eq=3.566 bohr

experimental_anchor.source: Le Roy et al. J. Chem. Phys. 142, 044305 (2015)

A_Iron_Martin_Na_compound_scaling.method: Halve max Iron-Martin CV deviation (2.3 kcal/mol for Mg+Br2) for single-Na NaH

B_Na2_scaling.method: Halve Na2 CV correction (~100-300 cm-1) for single-Na NaH

C_atomic_correlation_active_fraction.method: Total Na [Ne]-core correlation (~0.36 Ha) times active fraction (0.5-2%)

key_references.authors: Iron, M. A.; Oren, M.; Martin, J. M. L.
key_references.value: Subvalence correlation 'strongly recommended for Na'; reaction enthalpy CV up to 2.3 kcal/mol (Mg+Br2)
key_references.comment: Load-bearing reference for Na subvalence correlation ranking

key_references.authors: Sylvetsky, N.; Martin, J. M. L.
key_references.value: CV contributions to W4-17 atomization energies: 0.01-1.0 kcal/mol band; basis-set extrapolation RMSD 0.01 kcal/mol
key_references.comment: Quantitative scale for diatomic CV contributions

key_references.authors: Le Roy, R. J., et al.
key_references.value: NaH ground state D_e = 15797.4 +/- 4.3 cm-1 = 0.07198 Hartree
CV effects on Na/Mg reaction enthalpies; max single deviation 2.3 kcal/mol; most Na reactions > 1 kcal/mol

key_references.authors: Prascher, B. P.; Woon, D. E.; Peterson, K. A.; Dunning, T. H.; Wilson, A. K.
key_references.value: cc-pCVnZ basis sets for Li, Be, Na, Mg; recommended for accurate CV treatment
key_references.comment: Modern basis-set infrastructure for Na CV studies

key_references.value: alpha_d(Na+) approx 0.945 a.u., r_c approx 1.4 bohr (CPP for alkali hydrides)
key_references.comment: Core polarization potential scale for Na+ in molecular environment

gate_verdict_reasoning: Estimate 0.001-0.003 Ha is 2-3 orders of magnitude below DEFER threshold (0.1 Ha). Even upper bound 0.007 Ha is 14x below threshold. Core correlation contributes ~1% of experimental D_e, sub-kcal/mol scale; cannot close ~3.85 Ha structural wall.

honest_assessment.next_step: Schmidt orthogonalization (Track 1) is the only remaining sprint-scale architectural candidate with right order of magnitude; core correlation parks as Layer-2 polish for future precision-spectroscopy sprint
honest_assessment.literature_gap_flagged: No published explicit frozen-core vs all-electron CCSD(T) D_e differential for NaH at basis-set limit; CCCBDB has tabular data but was unreachable in this search
honest_assessment.residual_caveat: Estimate is for binding-relevant differential; absolute Na core correlation is ~0.36 Ha; if F6 wall is unexpectedly sensitive to absolute core treatment (unlikely given dissociation-reference behavior), revisit
